growth_records.strain: NRC-1 (Delta ura3 parent background; ChIP-seq harvest strains AKS134 empty-vector and KAD128 HpyA-HA)
growth_records.cite: Sakrikar et al. An archaeal histone-like protein regulates gene expression in response to salt stress. Nucleic Acids Research (2021). DOI:10.1093/nar/gkab1175. [PMC8682779]
growth_records.snippet: The 50 mL cultures were grown in 125 ml flasks and their growth was monitored by OD600 until the time for harvesting (exponential phase: 36-50 h, OD ~0.2-0.35, growth rate ~0.059 h-1; stationary phase: ~70-140 h, OD ~1.4-1.7, growth rate ~0
growth_records.notes: Prokaryote (archaeon) growth rate only; no substrate uptake, product secretion, or MFA fluxes reported (regulatory/ChIP-seq study). Reported exponential-phase mu ~0.059 h-1 in CM at 4.2 M NaCl (250 g/L NaCl = ~4.28 M), pH 6.8, 42 C, aerobic batch. Doubling time calculated: td = ln2/mu = 0.693/0.059 = 11.75 h. A decelerating stationary-phase rate ~0.032 h-1 (td=21.7 h) was also reported for the same culture but is not a true max growth rate. Separately, WT vs delta-hpyA mu_max were compared at optimal salt (4.2 M NaCl) and reduced salt (3.4 M NaCl): reduced salt slowed WT mu_max to 89% of the optimal-salt value, but absolute mu_max values were given only in Figure 1 (not in text) so are not captured here. Values are approximate (~) as printed. Other three papers in batch (PMC8670527 Arabidopsis root microbiome; PMC8692400 coral areal N2-fixation rates in ug N cm-2 d-1; PMC8692406 seafloor community 13C qSIP excess-atomic-fraction) report no pure-culture prokaryote uptake/secretion/growth-rate data in GEM-usable units and yield no records.

growth_records.cite: Sebastian Breuert et al. Regulated Polyploidy in Halophilic Archaea. PLoS ONE (2006). DOI:10.1371/journal.pone.0000092. [PMC1762399]
growth_records.snippet: The doubling time of H. salinarum grown aerobically in complex medium at 42C is about 4 hours... H. salinarum was grown by aerobic respiration at 42oC and 30 oC and by arginine fermentation at 42oC. The doubling times were 4 hours, 8 hours
growth_records.notes: Growth rate CALCULATED from reported doubling time: mu = ln2/td = 0.6931/4 h = 0.173/h. Medium described only as 'complex medium' with composition given by reference (Soppa 1998; Ruepp & Soppa 1996) - not stated in text, so medium_composition_stated:false. No substrate uptake, product secretion, or MFA fluxes reported anywhere in this polyploidy study. pH of growth medium not stated. This is the reference (fastest) aerobic condition.

growth_records.cite: Sebastian Breuert et al. Regulated Polyploidy in Halophilic Archaea. PLoS ONE (2006). DOI:10.1371/journal.pone.0000092. [PMC1762399]
growth_records.snippet: cultures were grown at 30C instead of 42C... In both cases, the doubling time is around 8 hours... H. salinarum was grown by aerobic respiration at 42oC and 30 oC... The doubling times were 4 hours, 8 hours and 8 hours, respectively.
growth_records.notes: Growth rate CALCULATED: mu = ln2/td = 0.6931/8 h = 0.0866 -> 0.087/h. Same organism/strain/medium as the 42C aerobic condition but grown at 30C, roughly doubling the doubling time. Medium composition given only by reference, not stated in text. No uptake/secretion/MFA data reported.

growth_records.strain: H. salinarum (strain not explicitly named in text)
growth_records.medium: defined amino-acid (synthetic complex) medium, salt-saturated (~4 M NaCl)

up.method: amino-acid depletion time-course, analytical (experimental data, diamonds with error bars, Fig 2)
depletion time-course; uptake accelerates near Ile depletion
depletion time-course; uptake accelerates near Leu depletion

sec.method: ~95% of consumed arginine exported via arginine-ornithine antiporter (experimental time-course, switches production->consumption)
carbamate kinase degradation of carbamoyl-phosphate + respiratory oxidation (inferred)

yields.biomass_amino_acid_content: ~503 ug amino acid / OD.ml (measured total AA content of biomass)

growth_records.cite: Gonzalez O et al. Systems Analysis of Bioenergetics and Growth of the Extreme Halophile Halobacterium salinarum. PLoS Computational Biology (2009). DOI:10.1371/journal.pcbi.1000332. [PMC2674319]
growth_records.snippet: While aerobic preparations exhibited exponential phase doubling times of only roughly 11 hours, phototrophic cultures demonstrated times close to 50 hours.
growth_records.notes: Halobacterium salinarum is an archaeon (prokaryote). Aerobic exponential-phase doubling time reported experimentally as ~11 h; mu calculated mu=ln(2)/td=0.6931/11=0.0630 h-1. Substrate uptake and product secretion are EXPERIMENTALLY measured time-courses (Fig 2, diamonds+error bars) but NOT tabulated as numeric mmol/gDW/h in text, so rate values left null. Qualitative priority ordering (highest uptake): arginine >> aspartate, isoleucine, leucine. Temperature and pH not stated in this paper. Medium is a defined amino-acid medium at salt-saturation (~4 M salt) with composition cited from prior work [15,23]; full composition not restated here.

growth_records.strain: H. salinarum (strain not explicitly named in text)
growth_records.medium: defined amino-acid (synthetic complex) medium, salt-saturated (~4 M NaCl)

up.units: not tabulated (modeled with same procedure as aerobic)
up.method: amino-acid depletion time-course (experimental phototrophy data, Fig S5)
depletion time-course; degraded even under phototrophy

sec.method: arginine fermentation product exported (occurs simultaneously with photosynthesis)

growth_records.cite: Gonzalez O et al. Systems Analysis of Bioenergetics and Growth of the Extreme Halophile Halobacterium salinarum. PLoS Computational Biology (2009). DOI:10.1371/journal.pcbi.1000332. [PMC2674319]
growth_records.snippet: Phototrophic cultures were grown under conditions similar to the aerobic preparations, except that oxygen was absent and they were illuminated with white light... phototrophic cultures demonstrated times close to 50 hours.
growth_records.notes: Phototrophic (anaerobic, light) batch of H. salinarum (archaeon). Experimental exponential-phase doubling time ~50 h; mu=ln(2)/td=0.6931/50=0.01386 h-1. Final OD >1.5, comparable to aerobic. Uptake/secretion measured as time-courses but not tabulated numerically; arginine fermentation shown to co-occur with photosynthesis. Temperature/pH not stated.
